summary: 2026-07-07 11:55:47 PDT - The branch implements the helper shape, cap/upsert behavior, and alias symmetry, but it still violates the hydration persistence contract in the spec/Build note. I also attempted the focused client Vitest run, but the read-only sandbox blocked Vite from writing `client/node_modules/.vite-temp`.

findings.title: Hydration still saves clean loads and double-saves repairs
findings.claim: AC4 and the Build note require clean loads to avoid a redundant save and dirty hydration repairs to be saved once. This load effect always calls `setPersisted(fixed)` for any server config, then flips `hydratedRef.current = true`; the normal persistence effect at lines 81-84 will schedule `saveRates(adapter, persisted)` for that just-loaded clean config. When `changed` is true, line 70 also calls `saveRates(adapter, fixed)` immediately, so the repaired config is written through both paths.
findings.suggestion: Add a load/save guard such as `lastSavedRef` or `skipNextPersistRef`: after hydration, mark the loaded or repaired config as already persisted so the debounced effect ignores that state. For dirty repairs, perform the one best-effort repair save and advance the same marker before enabling normal user-edit saves.
